page: Amazon Web Services c9g.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features an arm64 CPU architecture with 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides 384.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage solutions for data persistence. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of a graphics processing unit limits its applicability for hardware-accelerated rendering or deep learning workloads, while the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff that prioritizes raw integer and floating-point compute capacity over memory-intensive operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high compute density per resource unit. The instance is designed for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads. Suitable applications include large-scale web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, video encoding, and high-performance computing tasks that scale efficiently across many physical cores without requiring hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services c9g.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the arm64 architecture and AWS Graviton processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and focusing purely on CPU-driven tasks. With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, the instance is structured for high-throughput, parallel processing. The qualitative cost profile of this arm64-based hardware class makes it suitable for compute-heavy workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and large-scale web serving where high core density and dedicated physical execution units are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c9g.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 192 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for highly parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density arm64 compute instance featuring one hundred ninety-two dedicated physical cores and one hundred gigabits per second network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical arm64 cores running at 2.8 GHz.
384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Zero local storage and zero integrated GPUs, prioritizing pure CPU compute.
Engineered for batch processing, distributed analytics, and parallelized workloads.
